{"step_by_step_thinking": "The Frankfort horizontal (FH) plane is a standard reference plane in cephalometric analysis used to align the skull for measurement. It is defined as a line that connects the superior border of the external auditory meatus (porion) to the inferior border of the orbit (orbitale). This plane is crucial for assessing facial proportions and skeletal relationships. Among the provided options, the correct landmarks are Porion and Orbitale. Option D matches this definition. The other options (A: Nasion and Sella, B: Porion and Sella, C: Porion and Nasion) refer to other anatomical planes such as the sella-nasion line or the nasion-sella line, which are not the Frankfort horizontal. Document [24] from the MEDCORP source explicitly states: 'a well-established anatomical reference extending from the superior border of the external auditory canal to the inferior orbital rim.' This confirms that the Frankfort horizontal connects Porion (superior border of external auditory meatus) to Orbitale (inferior orbital rim). Therefore, the correct answer is D.", "answer_choice": "D"}
